response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a capable full-stack and systems developer with broad experience across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but he fundamentally does not meet the core minimum qualification for this role. The position requires 8 years of professional C++ experience, yet C++ does not appear in his skills section and his only documented C++ work is approximately 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor thesis project. His primary stack throughout his career has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While he has touched distributed systems and some AWS infrastructure at Dunnhumby and Optilyz, his experience falls well short of the 5-year large-scale infrastructure requirement, and there is no evidence of a formal technical leadership or cloud architect role.
strengths: Genuine distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O patterns in DLang
Demonstrated community leadership through conference talks, workshops, and open-source projects (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts), indicating mentoring instincts
AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes deployment of GISCollective show at least surface-level cloud and container familiarity
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2015) and the language is absent from his skills list entirely, failing the 8-year minimum by a massive margin
No evidence of a formal technical lead, staff-lead, or principal engineer role with cross-team ownership of a platform or large-scale infrastructure product
No cloud compute platform experience; AWS usage is limited to CDK for a mail automation pricing service, far from the infrastructure-layer depth the role demands
